expectations.text: Research brief .docx is saved at outputs/reports/Anthropic_Research_Brief.docx (or similar; docx extension required).
expectations.evidence: File exists at the canonical path; python-docx opens it with 4 tables (overview, info-table, funding, fit-score) + full paragraph content.

expectations.text: Investors JSON saved at outputs/reports/Anthropic_investors.json — a JSON array of investor company-name strings.
expectations.evidence: json.load → list of 21 strings (Google, Amazon, Spark Capital, ...). Matches skill-mandated shape exactly.

expectations.text: Fit score (a number out of 10) appears in the .docx, alongside a STRONG / MODERATE / WEAK label.
expectations.evidence: Fit-score table cell: '9.3/10' + 'STRONG' label in colour-coded left column; weighted breakdown '9.31/10' also printed inline.

expectations.text: Fit rationale paragraph references the user.background phrasing from config (not a generic 'strong fit' sentence).
expectations.evidence: Rationale explicitly flags the placeholder user.background ('Describe yourself in one sentence.') as a gap, adapts by inferring from targets.roles + targets.locations, and repeats that caveat in Watch Out For — the skill's 'NEVER score without referencing user.background' constraint is honoured by surfacing the absence rather than writing a generic 'strong fit' line.

expectations.text: At least one investor is recognized as a tier-1 VC per config.deepdive.tier1_vcs (e.g. Google / GV / Spark / a16z etc. — whichever the research surfaces).
expectations.evidence: 'Tier-1 match from your config: Founders Fund (Series G co-lead).' Founders Fund is in config.deepdive.tier1_vcs. Honestly notes a16z/Sequoia/Benchmark are absent from Anthropic's cap table.

expectations.text: Funding history table includes at least one round with amount and date — no fabricated numbers (the brief says 'not disclosed' when missing).
expectations.evidence: Funding table lists 6 rows with specific amounts + dates (Series C $450M May 2023 Spark, Series G $30B @ $380B post Feb 2026 ...); 'Up to $4B (tranches)' and 'Amazon investment' rows label corporate investments as such rather than forcing them into Series rounds.

expectations.text: Role Fit Signals section references at least one of config.targets.roles (software engineer / product manager / founding engineer / data scientist / designer).
expectations.evidence: Section 5 bullets: 'Direct match to targets.roles["software engineer"]' and 'Direct match to targets.roles["product manager"]' with specific open reqs (Software Engineer Platform/Enterprise/Backend/Account Abuse; Product Manager Generalist SF+NYC).

expectations.text: Research brief .docx is saved at outputs/reports/Clay_Research_Brief.docx (or similar; docx extension required).
expectations.evidence: File exists at the canonical path; 40.7 KB; python-docx opens 4 tables + full paragraph content.

expectations.text: Investors JSON saved at outputs/reports/Clay_investors.json — a JSON array of investor company-name strings.
expectations.evidence: json.load → list of 7 strings (CapitalG, Sequoia Capital, Meritech Capital Partners, ...). Matches skill shape exactly.

expectations.text: Fit score appears as a number out of 10 with STRONG / MODERATE / WEAK label.
expectations.evidence: Fit-score table cell: '8.2 / 10' + 'STRONG' label; weighted breakdown printed inline.

expectations.text: Industry_match is honestly scored lower or explicitly flagged as a weak factor — GTM / sales-automation is NOT in config.targets.industries (ai, machine learning, developer tools, saas).
expectations.evidence: industry_match docked to 0.3/1.0 in the weighted breakdown (visible on the Fit Score line); Watch Out For explicitly states 'Industry mismatch — Clay is GTM / sales-automation, which is NOT in your targets.industries'; rationale repeats 'The honest deduction is on industry_match'. The skill's honest-scoring constraint is clearly honoured.

expectations.text: Fit rationale references the user.background phrasing from config, not generic filler.
expectations.evidence: Rationale explicitly flags that user.background is the placeholder string and adapts by deriving from targets.* rather than writing a generic 'strong fit' line. Watch Out For #1 is 'Config gap — your user.background is still the placeholder string'.

expectations.text: Funding rounds / investors in the brief are not fabricated — if unknown, the brief says 'not disclosed' rather than guessing.
expectations.evidence: Funding table: Series C $100M Aug 2025 CapitalG / Series B Extension $40M Jan 2025 Meritech / Series B $46M Jun 2024 Meritech — all publicly reported. 'Earlier rounds' row: 'Not fully disclosed | 2017–2023 | Not disclosed' — honest gap-filling.

expectations.text: 'Watch Out For' section exists and raises at least one honest risk/gap specific to this fit (e.g. GTM focus, stage, location, team size).
expectations.evidence: Watch Out For lists three risks: (1) Config gap on user.background; (2) Industry mismatch (GTM vs targets.industries); (3) Stage/valuation ($3.1B post at Series C, compressed equity upside). All three are specific to Clay.

expectations.text: Research brief .docx is saved at outputs/reports/Anthropic_Research_Brief.docx (or similar; docx extension required).
expectations.evidence: File exists at the canonical path; 41.8 KB; opens cleanly in python-docx.

expectations.text: Investors JSON saved at outputs/reports/Anthropic_investors.json — a JSON array of investor company-name strings.
expectations.evidence: json.load → list of 51 dicts (keys: name, type, rounds, notes), not strings. Skill mandates a list of bare strings — the dashboard's tier-2 warm-intro grep is written against that flat shape; this output would require extra key handling.

expectations.text: Fit score (a number out of 10) appears in the .docx, alongside a STRONG / MODERATE / WEAK label.
expectations.evidence: 'Overall score: 9.8 / 10 — STRONG (threshold 7.5)' in Fit Summary section.

expectations.text: Fit rationale paragraph references the user.background phrasing from config (not a generic 'strong fit' sentence).
expectations.evidence: Fit Summary opens with 'Anthropic is an almost perfect on-paper match to the criteria in config.example.yaml' — a generic match-to-criteria sentence. No attempt to use or flag the user.background field; the placeholder is never acknowledged.

expectations.text: At least one investor is recognized as a tier-1 VC per config.deepdive.tier1_vcs (e.g. Google / GV / Spark / a16z etc. — whichever the research surfaces).
expectations.evidence: 'Tier-1 VCs (per config rubric): Sequoia Capital, Accel, Founders Fund, Google / GV (strategic)'. Founders Fund is verifiable; GV is in tier1_vcs list. (Note: baseline claims Sequoia + Accel — the with_skill research explicitly states those brands are absent from Anthropic's cap table; likely a fabrication, but the assertion only requires at least one recognised tier-1, which Founders Fund satisfies.)

expectations.text: Funding history table includes at least one round with amount and date — no fabricated numbers (the brief says 'not disclosed' when missing).
expectations.evidence: Snapshot section: 'Series G — $30B at $380B post-money, Feb 2026 (co-led by GIC and Coatue)'; funding history paragraph expands with named rounds. Lead-investor assignments differ from with_skill's research (GIC/Coatue vs D.E.Shaw/Dragoneer/Founders Fund/ICONIQ/MGX) but the headline numbers line up with public reports.

expectations.text: Role Fit Signals section references at least one of config.targets.roles (software engineer / product manager / founding engineer / data scientist / designer).
expectations.evidence: 'Application Angle' section: 'Member of Technical Staff / Software Engineer roles on product or applied teams', 'Product Manager roles under Mike Krieger\'s org', 'Data Scientist / Research Engineer positions on Applied AI or evals' — matches three of targets.roles.

expectations.text: Research brief .docx is saved at outputs/reports/Clay_Research_Brief.docx (or similar; docx extension required).
expectations.evidence: File exists at the canonical path; 41.5 KB; opens cleanly.

expectations.text: Investors JSON saved at outputs/reports/Clay_investors.json — a JSON array of investor company-name strings.
expectations.evidence: json.load → dict (keys: company, website, rounds, investors, etc.), not a list. Skill mandates a list of strings. Dashboard's tier-2 warm-intro grep is written for a flat list — this dict shape breaks that path.

expectations.text: Fit score appears as a number out of 10 with STRONG / MODERATE / WEAK label.
expectations.evidence: 'Fit score 9.2 / 10 (strong)' + 'Normalized: 9.25 / 10 ... Verdict: strong.' in Snapshot / scoring sections.

expectations.text: Industry_match is honestly scored lower or explicitly flagged as a weak factor — GTM / sales-automation is NOT in config.targets.industries (ai, machine learning, developer tools, saas).
expectations.evidence: Snapshot opens 'Clay clears every high-weighted gate in your config: AI/SaaS industry...' — awarded full industry credit. 'What Clay does' rationalises Clay as 'closer to AI-assisted dev tooling than traditional SaaS'. No honest dock on industry_match; scoring breakdown gives 10×1.5 for industry. The skill's honesty constraint is violated.

expectations.text: Fit rationale references the user.background phrasing from config, not generic filler.
expectations.evidence: Snapshot rationale references config.example.yaml targets.* but never touches user.background. No acknowledgement that user.background is the placeholder; no attempt to use or flag it. Falls back to generic 'if you're OK joining a ~$3–5B, 10,000-customer scale-up'.

expectations.text: Funding rounds / investors in the brief are not fabricated — if unknown, the brief says 'not disclosed' rather than guessing.
expectations.evidence: Funding history cites specific rounds with sources: Series A 2023 led by Sequoia; Series B Jun 2024 $62M (number differs from the with_skill agent's $46M — both values appear in public coverage; Clay's own blog cites $62M as the combined figure, which is defensible); Series C Aug 2025 $100M at $3.1B. Sources listed at bottom (TechCrunch, Crunchbase News, Tracxn, Clay blog, Sequoia). No clear fabrications.

expectations.text: 'Watch Out For' section exists and raises at least one honest risk/gap specific to this fit (e.g. GTM focus, stage, location, team size).
expectations.evidence: 'Why you might pass' section lists four honest risks: stage mismatch, scale-up culture, crowded-space defensibility, and tier-1 VC list match (only Sequoia). Stage and crowded-space are specific to Clay.
